text: UK Q1 Performance If you want to understand the macro backdrop, just look at the FTSE 100 winners and losers in Q1. FTSE 100 – Outperformers Beazley +52.4% Schroders +41.1% BP +38.7% Glencore +38.3% Shell +29.5% BAE +25.2% Centrica +24.2% SSE +19.0% Vodafone +15.0% BT +14.8% FTSE 100 – Underperformers Barratt Redrow –31.3% Entain –27.6% 3i –27.0% Metlen –26.9% ICG –26.0% Sage –22.3% Experian –22.0% Auto Trader –21.0% Persimmon –20.6% Intertek –20.0 Q1 Themes -Oil +51% since Feb following escalation in Iran. Commod exposed name higher - Defence continues to outperform on geopolitical tensions - UK M&A/bid activity pushed Beazley & Schroders higher - AI winners from 2024 continue to lag - Housebuilders weak amid macro uncertainty and rates FTSE 100 +2.75% YTD Euro Stoxx −4% YTD * thanks to my friend DB who works for an IB for the above colour.

text: I’m pleased to be starting my new role as Head of Capital Markets at The Smarter Web Company. I’ve been fortunate to be an investor in the company since the IPO and know how much Smarter Web means to many shareholders and why it’s important that we have a company built on digital capital listed in the UK. Based in London, my focus will be on making sure we are having the right conversations with investors, institutions, banks and partners, and that we are clearly communicating the Smarter Web equity story to maximise awareness. This includes building the right relationships so we can access capital to continue growing the Bitcoin treasury and support our long-term strategy. Over the past months I’ve spent a lot of time with @asjwebley and @Croesus_BTC and the wider team and it’s given me a real appreciation for how much work goes on behind the scenes and how committed everyone is to growing the business responsibly. I genuinely believe that if we continue executing on our 10-year plan, Smarter Web has the potential to become one of the largest companies in the UK. I’m grateful to Andrew for the trust he has placed in me, and I’m looking forward to contributing to the next stage of the company’s journey. We are still very early.

text: All great achievements that, in my view, will be reflected in the share price in due course. At the moment there is a disconnect between the share price and the underlying fundamentals for the higher quality treasury companies , which is largely being driven by broader Bitcoin and sector sentiment. What we seem to be learning is that during more challenging parts of the sentiment cycle, mNAV can trade at a deeper discount than expected, whereas during strong bull periods it tends to trade at a larger premium than expected. Keep doing what you guys are doing, block out the noise and trust the process!

text: Thank you for the update. Bitcoin treasury companies help address a simple but important question: “How do I get more Bitcoin?” Not everyone has large amounts of fiat to deploy, and even those who do may prefer not to self-custody or hold Bitcoin through an ETN. Instead, they want easy access to Bitcoin exposure through familiar market structures (potentially within tax efficient structures such as a SIPP or ISA) while increasing their exposure over the long term. This is where high quality, Bitcoin treasury companies become compelling. They provide a pathway for investors to gradually grow their Bitcoin exposure through the public markets. The compounding effect is particularly powerful. Even relatively small, consistent gains (when compounded over time) can have a tremendous impact on an investor’s overall Bitcoin exposure. Patience is key, but the long term results can be meaningful. Another factor contributing to the premium is the market’s confidence in management’s ability to execute its long term strategy and the initiatives aimed at increasing long term Bitcoin per share. Since IPO, $SWC has consistently demonstrated its ability to deliver on its 10-year plan, for example, the LSE uplisting, the Squarebird acquisition, and inclusion in the FTSE indices to name a few. As a shareholder, I was particularly pleased to see the inclusion in the FTSE All-Share and Small Cap indices. This represents a meaningful step forward in “Orange Pilling” the UK market, where many retail and institutional investors have historically missed out on Bitcoin exposure due to market access constraints. It was also constructive to see another strong week of trading volume in the stock. Regardless of short term price action (which we know can be volatile) it is encouraging to see a large number of shares changing hands, as this reflects continued attention and engagement from the market. Keep up the great work!

text: Another notable trading day for $SWC on the LSE: • ~£257k volume executed on the order book. • Equates to ~36% of total volume (assuming 33.2p closing price) For a market maker + CET supported line, rising order book participation is a key signal of liquidity maturing and price discovery strengthening. Constructive step for the London line.
